'''Atelea Vea''' is a [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Super League]]. He plays as a {{rlp|pr|lk}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward. He has previously played for the [[Melbourne Storm]], [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]] and the [[London Broncos]].

He made his first-grade début for the Sharks in [[2009 NRL season|2009]]. He was selected to play for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in the [[2009 Pacific Cup]].<ref name="rleague.com profile">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/player/v/vea_atelea/index.php|title=rleague.com profile|publisher=rleague.com|date=16 January 2010|accessdate=16 January 2010|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100113110246/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/v/vea_atelea/index.php|archivedate=13 January 2010|url-status=dead }}</ref>

==Background==
Vea was born in Sydney, Australia.

==Playing career==
===2009: Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks===
Vea made his first-grade début for the Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks away to the [[Penrith Panthers]], in a round 10 loss, a game in which he came on off the [[Rugby league positions#Substitutes|interchange bench]].<ref name="Panthers 26-22 Sharks">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/84000/84765.php|title=Panthers 26–22 Sharks|publisher=rleague.com|date=16 May 2009|accessdate=16 January 2010| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100103081312/http://rleague.com/db/content/84000/84765.php| archivedate= 3 January 2010 | url-status= live}}</ref> He then featured in the 26–4 loss at home to the St. George Illawarra Dragons in the next round, where he was again on the bench.<ref name="Sharks 4-26 Dragons">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/84000/84790.php|title=Sharks 4–26 Dragons|publisher=rleague.com|date=23 May 2009|accessdate=16 January 2010| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100103083746/http://rleague.com/db/content/84000/84790.php| archivedate= 3 January 2010 | url-status= live}}</ref> His first start for the Cronulla side came when the side hit something of a run of form. He started out in the second-row in a narrow 13–10 win over the [[Parramatta Eels]],<ref name="Eels 10-13 Sharks">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/84000/84797.php|title=Eels 10–13 Sharks|publisher=rleague.com|date=30 May 2009|accessdate=16 January 2010| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100103075845/http://rleague.com/db/content/84000/84797.php| archivedate= 3 January 2010 | url-status= live}}</ref> and he kept his starting place in wins over [[New Zealand Warriors]] (18–10),<ref name="Sharks 18-10 Warriors">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/84000/84836.php|title=Sharks 18–10 Warriors|publisher=rleague.com|date=7 June 2009|accessdate=16 January 2010| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100103081940/http://rleague.com/db/content/84000/84836.php| archivedate= 3 January 2010 | url-status= live}}</ref> [[Canberra Raiders]] (24–22)<ref name="Raiders 22-24 Sharks">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/84000/84797.php|title=Raiders 22–24 Sharks|publisher=rleague.com|date=14 June 2009|accessdate=16 January 2010| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100103075845/http://rleague.com/db/content/84000/84797.php| archivedate= 3 January 2010 | url-status= live}}</ref> and the thumping 46–16 thrashing of [[Brisbane Broncos]],<ref name="Sharks 46-12 Broncos">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/84000/84857.php|title=Sharks 46–12 Broncos|publisher=rleague.com|date=22 June 2009|accessdate=16 January 2010| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100103082909/http://rleague.com/db/content/84000/84857.php| archivedate= 3 January 2010 | url-status= live}}</ref> all of which were consecutive appearances. He was, in round 16 at the [[Sydney Roosters]], shuffled to lock, where the Sharks lost their first game in four.<ref name="Roosters 19-12 Sharks">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/84000/84797.php|title=Roosters 19–12 Sharks|publisher=rleague.com|date=27 June 2009|accessdate=16 January 2010| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100103075845/http://rleague.com/db/content/84000/84797.php| archivedate= 3 January 2010 | url-status= live}}</ref> He then had to wait another 10 rounds before being selected again, in the 26–24 loss to the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]].<ref name="Sharks 24-26 Rabbitohs">{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com/db/content/85000/85009.php|title=Sharks 24–26 Rabbitohs|publisher=rleague.com|date=5 September 2009|accessdate=16 January 2010| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100103075044/http://rleague.com/db/content/85000/85009.php| archivedate= 3 January 2010 | url-status= live}}</ref>

===2010–11: Melbourne Storm===
Signed by the [[Melbourne Storm]] in July 2009 ahead of the [[2010 NRL season]],<ref name="ABCsignings">{{cite web |title=Storm signs Stanley, Vea |url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-07-22/storm-signs-stanley-vea/1363296 |website=abc.net.au |publisher=Australian Broadcasting Corporation |access-date=30 June 2023 |date=22 July 2009}}</ref> Vea would have to bide his time in NSW Cup. He would return to NRL action in 2011, playing nine games for Melbourne, with all appearances of the bench. His performance in a losing effort in round 26 against the Sydney Roosters was praised by coach Craig Bellamy given the injury toll on the night.

===2012–13: St George-Illawarra===
He played for the St. George Illawarra Dragons in 2012 and 2013.

===2014: London===
He joined the [[London Broncos]] on 24 February 2014.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/?s=article-display&id=44676 |title=Dragons Recruit Vea For 2012 |date=23 October 2011 |work=www.dragons.com.au |accessdate=23 October 2011 |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303214149/http://www.dragons.com.au/?s=article-display&id=44676 |archivedate=3 March 2016 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

===2015-2016: St Helens===
In 2015, Vea joined St Helens where he made 40 appearances over two seasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/10606280/leigh-centurions-sign-forward-atelea-vea-from-st-helens-on-two-year-contract/|title=Leigh Centurions sign forward Atelea Vea from St Helens on two-year contract|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===2017: Leigh===
In 2017, Vea joined Leigh where he played 27 matches in the 2017 Super League season. Leigh would be relegated from the Super League after losing the [[Million Pound Game]] and Vea was released from his contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leigh-sign-atelea-vea-st-11989917/|title=Leigh sign Atelea Vea from St Helens|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk}}</ref>

==Representative career==
He made his international début for [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in a 44–14 defeat by [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]], where Vea scored a try.<ref name="PNG 44-14 Tonga">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/png-thrash-tonga-to-reach-pacific-cup-final/story-e6frexnr-1225791088830|title=PNG 44–14 Tonga|publisher=Daily Telegraph|date=25 October 2009|accessdate=16 January 2010}}</ref> As Tonga did not progress to the final, Vea's next game was in the third/fourth place play-off loss to [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]].<ref name="Fiji 26-16 Tonga">{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/fiji-beat-tonga-in-pacific-cup/story-e6frfgbo-1225793119996|title=Fiji 26–16 Tonga|publisher=Herald Sun|date=31 October 2009|accessdate=16 January 2010}}</ref>
